SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.propagator

Interface ContextPropagator

All Known Implementing Classes:
CompositeContextPropagator, MdcContextPropagator, MicrometerContextPropagator, TenantContextPropagator
Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface ContextPropagator
Strategy for propagating thread-bound context (tenant ID, security principal, MDC trace) across executor boundaries in scatter/gather operations.

Implementations capture context on the calling (request) thread when wrap(java.lang.Runnable) is invoked, then restore it before executing the task on the executor thread.

The default no-op implementation (noOp()) is suitable when no thread-bound context needs to be propagated. Applications using a ThreadLocal-based tenant context (e.g. TenantContext) should supply a custom implementation.

Example — tenant propagator


 ContextPropagator tenantPropagator = task -> {
     String tenantId = TenantContext.get();          // captured on the calling thread
     return () -> {
         if (tenantId != null) TenantContext.set(tenantId);
         try { task.run(); } finally { TenantContext.clear(); }
     };
 };
Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:
  • ScatterGatherFailoverHandler

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static @NonNull ContextPropagator
    noOp()
    No-op propagator — passes tasks through unchanged.
    @NonNull Runnable
    wrap(@NonNull Runnable task)
    Captures the current thread context and returns a Runnable that restores it before running task on the executor thread.
    default <V> Supplier<V>
    wrapSupplier(Supplier<V> task)
    Variant of wrap(java.lang.Runnable) for tasks that produce a value.

  • Method Details

    • wrap

      @NonNull Runnable wrap(@NonNull Runnable task)
      Captures the current thread context and returns a Runnable that restores it before running task on the executor thread.

      Must be called on the originating (request) thread so that context is captured before the executor boundary is crossed.

      Parameters:
      task - the task to wrap with context propagator
      Returns:
      a context-restoring wrapper around task

    • wrapSupplier

      default <V> Supplier<V> wrapSupplier(Supplier<V> task)
      Variant of wrap(java.lang.Runnable) for tasks that produce a value.

      Context is captured when this method is called (on the request thread). The returned Supplier restores context and produces the value on the executor thread.

      Type Parameters:
      V - the return type
      Parameters:
      task - the value-producing task to wrap
      Returns:
      a context-restoring wrapper around task

    • noOp

      static @NonNull ContextPropagator noOp()
      No-op propagator — passes tasks through unchanged. Suitable when no thread-bound context needs to be propagated.
      Returns:
      a propagator that returns the task unchanged
